responseMessageContents.text: The page is showing the **old** IP `35.252.150.63`. The current tunnel IP is different. Let me check what IP is currently being reported and give you the correct one.

input.command:
curl -sI https://lazy-beans-count.loca.lt/app-release.apk 2>/dev/null | grep -i "x-localtunnel-agent-ips\|x-robots\|HTTP" | head -5

echo ""
echo "=== Current public IP of sandbox ==="
curl -s ifconfig.me 2>/dev/null
echo ""
curl -s icanhazip.com 2>/dev/null
echo ""

echo "=== Check if tunnel is still active ==="
kill -0 $(cat /tmp/lt-pid.txt 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null && echo "Tunnel OK" || echo "Tunnel dead"

echo ""
echo "=== Try to get current IP from tunnel output ==="
cat /tmp/lt-output.txt 2>/dev/null
echo ""
echo "If tunnel is dead, create fresh one..."
if ! kill -0 $(cat /tmp/lt-pid.txt 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null; then
  cat /tmp/lt-pid.txt 2>/dev/null | xargs -r kill 2>/dev/null || true
  sleep 2
  bunx --yes localtunnel --port 8080 > /tmp/lt-output.txt 2>&1 &
  echo $! > /tmp/lt-pid.txt
  sleep 12
  cat /tmp/lt-output.txt
  NEW_URL=$(grep -oP 'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.loca\.lt' /tmp/lt-output.txt | head -1)
  echo ""
  if [ -n "$NEW_URL" ]; then
    echo "NEW URL: $NEW_URL/app-release.apk"
    curl -sI "$NEW_URL/app-release.apk" | grep -i "x-localtunnel-agent-ips\|HTTP" | head -3
  fi
fi

responseMessageContents.text:
The correct IP is **136.66.153.202**.

The page you saw still has the old IP `35.252.150.63` pre-filled. Clear it and enter:

**136.66.153.202**

Then tap **Continue**.

Then it will download the APK.
